findings.claim: Every tenth swap transfers 1 ether of the output token from contract reserves to the caller, allowing pools to be completely drained using repeating low-value swaps.

route.rationale: Draining 1 ether on every tenth swap directly violates the constant product invariant verified by check_swapPreservesXYK.

steps.stdout_excerpt:
Running 1 tests for test/Invariants.t.sol:Invariants
Counterexample: 
    p_inputAmount_uint64_c7e6fcd_00 = 0x800000000000000
[FAIL] check_swapPreservesXYK(uint64) (paths: 7, time: 0.55s, bounds: [])
Symbolic test result: 0 passed; 1 failed; time: 0.65s

findings.claim: The function incorrectly passes the input pool token amount as the output WETH amount to swapExactOutput, enabling users to drain WETH from the pool.

route.rationale: The parameter mismatch in the sellPoolTokens wrapper function is not directly covered by the core swap XYK invariant.

verification.note: no formal tool fit — escalated to human

findings.claim: Use of 10000 instead of 1000 as the scaling multiplier causes the calculated input amount to be ten times larger than required.

route.rationale: The use of an incorrect constant multiplier (10000 instead of 1000) is a logic/scaling bug that does not fit narrow integer-casting or width-independent arithmetic solvers.

verification.note: no formal tool fit — escalated to human
